name: KVM
summary: Capture the screen, drive keyboard/mouse, and ground UI targets (locateâ†’clickâ†’verify) through kvm:// routes â€” cross-platform, auto-selecting the best backend.
description: KVM is an external urirun connector for screen capture, keyboard/mouse control, and UI grounding on Linux (Wayland and X11), Windows and macOS. Every capability is served by a decorator-registered backend; at call time the connector picks the highest-priority backend available on the live session (XDG portal/grim on Wayland, mss/Pillow elsewhere for capture; ydotool/wtype/xdotool/pynput for input; AT-SPI/EasyOCR/tesseract/imgl for locating UI elements) and falls through on failure. On top of the input primitives it adds a perceiveâ†’locateâ†’actâ†’verify layer (ui/* + a11y), a focus-crop capture, and a pixel-accurate absolute click â€” the building blocks for vision/computer-use agents. Run kvm://host/doctor/query/report to see what is available and what to install.
useCases: Drive a remote desktop's keyboard/mouse from a URI flow, on any OS.
Capture a screenshot of a host (Wayland portal included), or a zoomed focus crop, as part of an automation.
Locate a UI element by text/role and click it (ui/command/click-text, a11y/command/act) â€” OCR/accessibility grounding for vision agents.
Run a bounded focusâ†’clickâ†’typeâ†’submit sequence in one session via task/command/run.
Project KVM control to MCP/A2A so agents can operate a desktop.
